# OPC UA PHP Client

> Pure PHP OPC UA client library. Communicates directly over TCP using the OPC UA binary protocol. No external C/C++ dependencies — only ext-openssl required.

## What is this

A PHP library for connecting to OPC UA servers (industrial automation protocol). It handles the full communication stack: TCP transport, binary encoding, secure channels, sessions, and all major OPC UA services. Supports PHP 8.2+.

## Use cases

- Read and write process variables from PLCs, SCADA systems, sensors, historians
- Browse the OPC UA address space programmatically
- Call OPC UA methods with typed arguments
- Subscribe to data changes and events
- Query historical data (raw, processed, at-time)
- Integrate industrial data into PHP/Laravel applications

## Key features

- Browse: recursive browsing, automatic continuation, tree building
- Path Resolution: resolve human-readable paths like /Objects/MyPLC/Temperature to NodeIds
- Read/Write: single and multi operations with all OPC UA data types
- Method Call: invoke OPC UA methods with typed arguments
- Subscriptions: data change and event monitoring with publish/acknowledge
- History Read: raw, processed (aggregated), and at-time historical queries
- Endpoint Discovery: discover available server endpoints and security policies
- Security: 6 policies (None, Basic128Rsa15, Basic256, Basic256Sha256, Aes128Sha256RsaOaep, Aes256Sha256RsaPss)
- Authentication: Anonymous, Username/Password, X.509 Certificate
- Auto-Retry: automatic reconnect on connection failures
- Auto-Batching: transparent batching for readMulti/writeMulti with server limits discovery
- ExtensionObject Codecs: pluggable codec system for custom OPC UA structures
- Connection State: lifecycle tracking (Disconnected, Connected, Broken) with reconnect()

## Architecture

The library is organized in layers:

- Client (src/Client.php): main entry point, implements OpcUaClientInterface, uses 13 traits for functionality
- Transport (src/Transport/): TCP socket communication
- Protocol (src/Protocol/): OPC UA service encoding/decoding (Read, Write, Browse, Call, Subscribe, etc.)
- Encoding (src/Encoding/): binary serialization (BinaryEncoder, BinaryDecoder)
- Security (src/Security/): secure channel, crypto operations, certificate management
- Types (src/Types/): OPC UA data types (NodeId, Variant, DataValue, BuiltinType, etc.)
- Exception (src/Exception/): exception hierarchy (Connection, Protocol, Security, Service, Encoding, Configuration)

## Installation

```
composer require gianfriaur/opcua-php-client
```

## Quick example

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Client;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\NodeId;

$client = new Client();
$client->connect('opc.tcp://localhost:4840');

$value = $client->read(NodeId::numeric(0, 2259));
echo $value->getValue();

$refs = $client->browse(NodeId::numeric(0, 85));
foreach ($refs as $ref) {
    echo $ref->getDisplayName() . "\n";
}

$client->disconnect();
```

## Secure connection example

```php
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Client;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityPolicy;
use Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityMode;

$client = new Client();
$client->setSecurityPolicy(SecurityPolicy::Basic256Sha256);
$client->setSecurityMode(SecurityMode::SignAndEncrypt);
$client->setClientCertificate('/certs/client.pem', '/certs/client.key', '/certs/ca.pem');
$client->setUserCredentials('operator', 'secret');
$client->connect('opc.tcp://192.168.1.100:4840');
```

## Main classes

- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Client — main client class
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\OpcUaClientInterface — public API contract
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\NodeId — OPC UA node identifier (numeric, string, guid, opaque)
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\Variant — typed value container
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\DataValue — value with status code and timestamps
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BuiltinType — enum of 25 OPC UA primitive types
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Types\BrowseNode — tree node for recursive browse results
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityPolicy — enum of 6 security policies
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Security\SecurityMode — enum (None, Sign, SignAndEncrypt)
- Gianfriaur\OpcuaPhpClient\Encoding\ExtensionObjectCodec — interface for custom type codecs

## Related packages

- gianfriaur/opcua-php-client-session-manager: session persistence across PHP requests
- gianfriaur/opcua-laravel-client: Laravel integration (service provider, facade, config)
- GianfriAur/opcua-test-server-suite: Docker-based OPC UA test servers

## Alternatives and comparison

### PHP alternatives

- techdock/opcua (https://github.com/TECHDOCK-CH/php-opc-ua): PHP 8.4+, binary protocol, requires phpseclib + symfony/cache + monolog + brick/date-time + wapmorgan/binary-stream. Supports Basic256Sha256, no history read, no auto-batching. Still at v0.2.
- techdock/opcua-webapi-client: PHP 8.1+, HTTP-based (not binary protocol), requires an OPC UA WebAPI gateway. Very early (v0.1).
- QuickOPC (OPC Labs): commercial, Windows-only, uses COM interop. Not a Composer package.

### Why this library over alternatives

- Only ext-openssl required (zero Composer dependencies in production)
- PHP 8.2+ support (wider compatibility than techdock which requires 8.4+)
- 6 security policies (None, Basic128Rsa15, Basic256, Basic256Sha256, Aes128Sha256RsaOaep, Aes256Sha256RsaPss)
- History read support (raw, processed, at-time)
- Auto-batching with server limits discovery
- Works on Linux, no Windows/COM dependency
- Native binary protocol (no HTTP gateway needed)
- Laravel integration available via gianfriaur/opcua-laravel-client

### Cross-language alternatives

- node-opcua (TypeScript/Node.js): most mature open-source OPC UA lib, client + server, MIT
- opcua-asyncio (Python): async client + server, LGPL-3.0
- UA-.NETStandard (C#): OPC Foundation reference implementation, client + server
- gopcua (Go): pure Go client + server, MIT
- open62541 (C): most widely used C implementation, client + server, MPL-2.0

The value of this library: if your stack is PHP, you get a native OPC UA client without spinning up a sidecar in another language or depending on COM/FFI/gateways.

## Requirements

- PHP >= 8.2
- ext-openssl

## License

MIT
